Ad Text:
APS6/EL1 Business Analyst


3-month contract until end of June
Canberra-based
Baseline clearance required 
Role Overview:
The role will focus on evaluating the system's current functionality, consulting with key stakeholders to understand operational needs, identifying areas for improvement, and documenting recommendations for system and process enhancements. The Business Analyst will also develop updated process maps to reflect current and proposed workflows.

Key Responsibilities:

Conduct a detailed review of the existing internal system, including its current functionality and performance.
Analyse the system's three core functions and assess whether they continue to meet current operational requirements.
Engage and consult with internal stakeholders to gather feedback on system usage, challenges, and opportunities for improvement.
Identify gaps, inefficiencies, and areas where processes or system functionality can be enhanced.
Document findings in a clear review report outlining current state analysis and recommended improvements.
Develop and update process maps to accurately reflect both current workflows and proposed future-state processes.
Provide practical recommendations for system enhancements, process improvements, or potential upgrades.
Present findings and recommendations to relevant internal stakeholders.
Skills and Experience:

Proven experience as a Business Analyst, particularly in system or process reviews.
Strong stakeholder engagement and consultation skills.
Experience in process mapping and business process improvement.
Ability to analyse systems and translate findings into clear recommendations.
Strong documentation and report writing skills.
Experience working with internal systems reviews or transformation projects is desirable.
How to Apply
To apply, click the Apply Now button and send your up-to-date resume, for a confidential discussion call us now on 1300 111 365.

Applications received after submission may be considered for similar roles.

If this job isn't quite right for you but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.

What success looks like in this role:

• Leads large, complex and geographically expansive client implementations.
• Establishes, leads and partners with the Service Delivery organization to meet schedules and resolve technical and operational problems.
• Ensures the successful planning, implementation and execution of contracted services by:
 o Meeting cost, governance, performance, quality, innovation and value requirements,
 o Participating in contract and account governance,
 o Developing or co-developing annual review of service level agreements.
• Effectively interprets complex business requirements and uses them to design and execute improved client solutions.
• Proactively communicates industry and company insights and opportunities.
• Establishes credibility with clients as a senior thought partner who can help solve their process, operational or technical needs.
Provides operational and risk management strategies across accounts and ensures these are adapted and applied by service delivery teams.




You will be successful in this role if you have:

Bachelor's degree and 8+ years’ relevant experience OR equivalent combination of education and experience
Master’s degree preferred

Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst - Canberra or Sydney - Hybrid
Ad Text:
Your new role

The Senior Finance Analyst plays a key role in supporting CSC’s strategic and operational objectives by delivering high‑quality financial analysis, reporting and insight across the organisation. Working closely with the Finance Business Partners, you will provide timely, accurate and practical insights that enable confident decision‑making across business, projects and major transformation initiatives.

Analytically strong and naturally curious, you will enjoy asking the right questions and translating complex data into meaningful insights. You will provide dedicated analytical capability to support portfolio level cost transparency, improve forecasting accuracy and drive proactive financial management. As a trusted team player with strong problem solving skills, you will also contribute to broader Finance Business Partnering activities by providing analytical support across portfolios as required.

Key responsibilities:

Prepare and analyse financial reporting for spend across BAU, projects and transformation programs, ensuring outputs are accurate, timely and clearly explained.
Support the preparation of forecasts, scenarios and multi-year financial plans, contributing to improved accuracy and forward looking insight.
Analyse cost drivers, trends and variances, proactively identifying emerging risks, pressures and opportunities for cost management and optimisation.
Build, maintain and enhance cost models to support funding proposals, business cases, benefit tracking and Executive decision papers.
Assist with month end and budget processes, including review of coding accuracy, reconciliations, accruals, capitalisation and project allocations.
Work collaboratively with the Finance Business Partners and the broader Finance team to improve data quality, reporting processes and analytical capability.

What you’ll need to excel

Degree from a recognised tertiary institution in Finance, Accounting or Commerce
Appropriate professional qualification (e.g. CA, CPA or equivalent), or a relevant non-accounting qualification (e.g. an applicable MBA) is desirable
Experience with large financial management information systems (e.g. Technology One, SAP, Oracle, etc.) is desirable
Strong commercial acumen with proven ability to partner and influence at Executive level
Clear, confident communicator with experience providing practical, data‑driven insights
Experience delivering high‑quality reporting for senior executives and/or Boards
High attention to detail with strong numerical and analytical capability
Solid experience in budgeting, forecasting and variance analysis
Background in financial services and/or project environments
High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office suite, including Outlook, Word, and PowerPoint, with proficiency in Excel

Why join CSC?

A permanent full-time role which offers hybrid working arrangements from our Sydney or Canberra office.
Join high‑performing, inclusive team where development, growth and progression are actively encouraged. Leaders are invested in your success and create a culture of opportunity.
Be part of a collaborative team committed to enhancing the quality of reporting and analysis through a mindset of continuous improvement, growing alongside the function as it evolves.

How to apply

To apply for this opportunity, please submit your CV via the Apply Link in this advertisement.

To ensure all applications are considered in the review process, we request you to please apply through the advertised links only.

Applications close: 7 April 2026

For any other questions about this role, contact recruitment@csc.gov.au
Agency submissions will not be accepted.

What you’ll get in return

At CSC, we’re committed to more than super — we’re committed to people. That means creating a workplace that values diversity, promotes equity, and fosters inclusion across everything we do.

Our gender equality strategy is more than a promise — it’s a plan. We're actively increasing representation in our workforce, removing barriers to career progression, and creating pathways for all employees to grow and succeed.

We welcome candidates of all genders, cultural backgrounds, ages, sexualities, and abilities. We offer flexible work, inclusive policies, and development opportunities designed to support you at every stage of your career.

We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer — and proud of the progress we’re making.

Join a workplace where you can thrive, grow, and belong. Learn more about CSC and our benefits:
www.csc.gov.au/Members/About-CSC/Careers

Position Description: Energy Market Analyst

Location: Brisbane, Queensland

Reports to: Director, Networks

Company Overview:

Blackstart is a dynamic and growing professional services firm recognised for its expertise in navigating the technical, commercial, and regulatory complexities of the energy transition. Our work spans the Australian and South-East Asian energy markets, supporting high impact projects across generation, transmission, storage, and large energy users. We pride ourselves on a collaborative environment, meaningful professional development opportunities, and a commitment to innovation.

Role Overview:

As an Energy Market Analyst, you will play a central role in modelling, analysing, and interpreting energy market dynamics to support client projects and strategic decision making. You will develop and run electricity market models, assess project economics, analyse market scenarios, and contribute to commercial and regulatory insights.

Key Responsibilities:

Energy Market Modelling & Analysis:

- Build, calibrate, and run energy market models to assess dispatch outcomes, price forecasts, and market behaviour.

- Develop long term market scenarios including sensitivities.

- Analyse results to support project feasibility, investment cases, network connection strategies, and commercial assessments.

- Interpret model outputs into insights suitable for technical and executive audiences.

Commercial & Financial Insight:

- Support project valuation and commercial modelling across renewable, storage, hydrogen, and hybrid assets.

- Assess revenue streams such as arbitrage, FCAS, capacity products, PPAs, and emerging markets.

- Identify market risks and opportunities across investment horizons.

Regulatory & Policy Analysis:

- Monitor regulatory developments across the NEM and WEM

- Assess impacts of policy and rule changes on client strategy and market outcomes.

- Contribute to submissions and strategy papers.

Reporting & Communication:

- Prepare high‑quality reports, presentations, dashboards, and visualisations.

- Present findings to clients and internal stakeholders.

- Communicate complex modelling concepts in clear, plain language.

Client Engagement & Collaboration:

- Work with clients to understand project needs and modelling assumptions.

- Collaborate with technical specialists, and commercial analysts.

- Support proposal development and study delivery.

Innovation & Continuous Improvement:

- Recommend improvements to modelling workflows and datasets.

- Stay up to date with emerging technologies and market frameworks.

- Support internal capability building.

Qualifications & Experience:

Education:

- Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Economics, Mathematics, Data Science, or related field; a Master's degree is highly desirable.

Technical & Analytical Skills:

- Strong quantitative skills with experience in market or power system modelling.

- Experience with PLEXOS, PSO, Aurora, BID3, or similar tools. Experience with PLEXOS is highly advantageous.

- Proficiency in Python, R, VBA, and SQL desirable.

Industry Experience:

- 2–6 years in electricity markets, energy economics, market modelling, or similar fields.

- Prior energy sector experience is a requirement for the role.

- Familiarity with NEM structure and regulatory frameworks.

- Working knowledge of Australian energy regulatory requirements is required for the role.

Communication & Collaboration:

- Excellent written and verbal communication.

- Ability to manage competing priorities and work in multidisciplinary teams.

Why Blackstart?

At Blackstart you will use your hard-earned skills to make a meaningful impact on the real-world challenges that need to be overcome to enable the energy transition. Blackstart are committed to sustainable personal and professional growth and believe that business objectives must be harmonised with this growth.

Career Growth: Opportunities to work on high-impact projects that shape the energy sector in Australia.

Relocation Support: Relocation support packages for candidates moving from overseas, including visa assistance and support with settling in Australia.

Balance: Flexible working arrangements and a commitment to maintaining a healthy balance to achieve your personal goals as well as those of Blackstart.

Healthy Work Environment: Work in a healthy and inclusive environment that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning.

Solid understanding of Agile delivery principles and how to apply them pragmatically in complex organisations.

This role requires a strong mix of technical expertise and business acumen, applying tools such as Databricks, SQL, Python, or R to design, deploy, and refine predictive models and scorecards. Skilled at influencing and presenting to senior management; experience leading initiatives across Product, Sales, Commercial, and Corporate functions.

Job description:



About VIDA Health

VIDA Health (Victorian Health Building Authority) is responsible for the rollout of the Victorian Government's multi-billion-dollar health capital projects. We're delivering world-class health facilities across Victoria including new public hospitals and emergency care, community health services, ambulance branches, residential aged care and mental health facilities. VIDA Health also provides technical support across engineering, property, asset management and environmental sustainability disciplines.

The Opportunity

As Manager, Project Data Governance, you will lead a team of analysts within the Project Controls branch to ensure project data is accurate, consistent, well-governed and fit for purpose. Your work will underpin critical internal and external reporting to senior executives, Ministers, central agencies and the public.

This is a senior leadership role focused on establishing strong data governance frameworks, driving quality and assurance across project reporting, and enabling meaningful insights through modern analytics and visualisation tools.

Working closely with project teams, Reporting & Analytics, and key stakeholders across VIDA and government, you will ensure project data supports informed decision-making across the full project lifecycle.


Key aspects of the role include:

Establishing and maintaining data governance standards, definitions and quality frameworks for project data.
Leading a team of analysts to deliver high-quality insights and dashboards using tools such as Power BI.
Overseeing the coordination, quality assurance and delivery of monthly project and program reporting.
Supporting external reporting, ministerial briefs and audit requirements with defensible, accurate data.
Driving innovation and continuous improvement across project data, reporting and governance practices.
Ensuring compliance with legislative, privacy and information security requirements.

We are looking for:

A data governance or reporting leader with experience in complex, enterprise-scale environments.
Strong expertise in data quality, governance frameworks and assurance processes
Experience working with analytics and reporting platforms such as Power BI, Excel/Power Query, SharePoint and SQL-based environments.
Proven ability to translate complex data into clear insights for executive and non-technical audiences.
A confident leader who builds capability, influences stakeholders and drives continuous improvement.

Tertiary qualification in data governance, business, analytics, risk, project management or a related discipline, or demonstrated equivalent professional experience.

AEMO at the Heart of Energy

 AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this.

We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection.

 Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector!

 About the Team & the Position

 The System Design Division plays a critical role in AEMO’s success, driving the organisation’s collaborative and industry-wide efforts in meeting the challenges of a transitioning energy system. The Division includes specialist areas in system planning, forecasting, future energy systems as well as onboarding and connections.

 The Market Operability team is seeking a Principal Analyst to undertake advanced energy modelling and analysis for the east coast gas and electricity systems. The team is responsible for the delivery of Gas Statement of Opportunities (GSOO), operability forecasting for the Integrated System Plan (ISP) for the National Electricity Market (NEM) and integrated gas and electricity modelling for the ISP.

 As a Principal Analyst, you will be responsible for providing strategic direction to key reports, overseeing modelling and analysis, and communicating results to various internal and external stakeholders. You will use a mix of forecast methods for the gas and electricity markets and modelling techniques focused on system optimisation over the short, medium and long-term. The Principal Analyst will lead model developments and improvements affecting short-term modelling to better analyse the reliability of the future energy system and support the development of the ISP

 Position Responsibilities:

Provide expert technical advice, expertise, and leadership on market modelling matters, including coordination of the GSOO process, leading short-term market modelling components of the ISP.

Apply advanced modelling methods, economic theory, and/or technical engineering knowledge to finalise model inputs and assumptions, run models, analyse results, and present analysis in written reports and verbal presentations.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

Support the Manager in people-management related processes, such as recruitment and development of staff, training, and regular feedback and performance appraisals.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

 Position Requirements

Skills & Experience:

Proven experience in energy market simulation software (preferably PLEXOS) and forecasting tools and processes.

Demonstrated experience in energy market analysis (electricity and/or gas) and the interpretation of analysis performed by others.

Demonstrable experience in managing projects and people to achieve timely delivery of specified outcomes.

Knowledge and experience in analysing the physical operation of the electricity and/or gas systems and the overarching markets, and

understanding of power system reliability assessments.

Experience in writing technical reports and other documents that convey, in simple terms, complex ideas and analysis.

Very strong communication skills, particularly the ability to present modelling and analysis, and make recommendations to internal and external stakeholders

Education/ professional Certifications: 

Tertiary and/or post graduate qualifications in mathematics, economics, data science, physics or engineering or an appropriate technology discipline.

 In return some of our benefits to you

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more.

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility).

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP). 

 About Our Process 

At AEMO we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome people of all backgrounds, including people with disabilities and neurodivergence and those who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander.

If you have any accessibility requirements to support you during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing us at talent@aemo.com.au

Our client is embarking on a significant enterprise-wide transformation program designed to modernise its technology landscape and enhance how its stakeholders engage with the organisation. At Lite n' Easy our goal is to create a healthier Australia by changing the way Australians eat. We make it easy for people to eat well, whether they want to lose weight or simply stay healthy. As a quality driven food company, all of our creative energies go into making our meals as delicious as they are healthy.




Are you a Lean practitioner with a passion for weaving together human capability and cutting-edge technology? Lite n' Easy is looking for a specialist to spearhead our transformation journey in Brisbane.

This is newly created role is designed for a forward-thinker who can bridge the gap between today’s processes and tomorrow’s possibilities. You won’t just be managing projects; you will be the designer of our automation roadmap, partnering with senior leadership to foster a high-performance culture that thrives on innovation and steady progress.

The Role:

Reporting to the Operations Manager (QLD/NSW), you will be the driving force behind site-wide evolution:

Automation Strategy: Lead the vision for our digital future. You will oversee the integration of robotics and vision systems, guiding the journey from initial concept to the seamless commissioning of advanced technologies

Lean Leadership: Act as the site’s CI mentor. You’ll establish Lean frameworks (5S, Kanban, SMED) and facilitate Kaizen events, empowering our frontline teams to take ownership of their own efficiency and growth.

Operational Impact: Take ownership of site-wide health. Your success is measured by the tangible smoothing of workflows, the elimination of waste, and the sustained improvement of our manufacturing and supply chain agility.

Digital Insights: Translate complex operational data into clear, actionable stories. Using Power BI and advanced analytics, you will provide the roadmap that helps the business understand the long-term value of our automation investments.

About you

The Experience: A deep background in automated supply chain environments, specifically with a "boots on the ground" understanding of commissioning sophisticated picking and sorting systems.

The Methodology: A Six Sigma mindset (Green Belt or higher) with a proven ability to lead cross-functional teams through the nuances of a Lean rollout.

The Technical Edge: A natural comfort with Power BI and a working curiosity for the interplay between PLC/SCADA systems and vision technology.

The Influence: A communicator who builds bridges someone just as effective coaching on the shopfloor as they are articulating a strategic vision to the executive team.

The Foundation: A holistic understanding of how operational efficiency intersects with WHS and HACCP standards.

Why Lite n' Easy?

Shape the Future: Have a direct hand in defining what our digital and automation strategy looks like from the ground up.

Hybrid Flexibility: Enjoy a modern, Brisbane-based working model that respects your life outside of work.

High Visibility: Gain direct exposure to the Senior Leadership Team in a role where your autonomy and ideas carry significant weight.

If you're excited to take on your next challenge, lead from the front and contribute to one of Australia’s leading food companies, Apply now! Join us in shaping the future of healthy eating in Australia.  Candidates must have full work rights in Australia – Australian Citizen or New Zealand Citizen.

Develop financial models and scenario analysis to support budgets, forecasts, long-range planning and investment decisions.

About You

You are a commercially minded analyst with strong analytical skills and the ability to translate complex data into clear insights. You are comfortable working across finance, operations and strategy, and you enjoy supporting practical decision-making and engaging with senior stakeholders. Sound commercial judgement and the ability to test assumptions and evaluate trade-offs.

Life at Hewitt

Hewitt is a global, vertically integrated foods business on a mission to feed the world sustainably. While our roots are deep in Australian soil, with a 120-year family history, guided by a simple philosophy: treat the land well, treat people well, and treat animals well. We are growing internationally, supplying nutritious, ethical, and organic meat to high-profile retailers and premium foodservice operators across Australia, the United States, and Asia.

We are not only an agricultural company, but we are also a premium food supplier connecting discerning consumers with high-claims, sustainable protein through our complete end-to-end supply chain, from our own cattle stations and select producer partners to processing and retail.

Hewitt is a hands-on, collaborative business where being present matters.  Whether in the office, on station, or in production, you’re contributing to something real: real food, real impact, real teamwork.

Join Hewitt for the Opportunity of a Lifetime

Opportunities to grow and develop – Learn on the job, gain new skills, and progress across office, production, and farm/station roles.

The Hewitt Experience – Shared Connections & Celebration – Our teams come together to celebrate wins and build connections. Enjoy monthly BBQs cooked with our finest meats, make-your-own wrap or sandwich, morning teas, staff sales, and Employee of the Month recognition. Whether you’re in the office, on production & warehouse sites, or working at our cattle and sheep stations, there’s always an opportunity to connect, celebrate, and enjoy being part of a tight-knit team.

The role

Align information technology systems with business operations through the analysis of internal standards, legacy system capabilities (AS/400), and modern DevOps practices.
Work effectively and autonomously with highly technical stakeholders (Developers, Architects, DevOps Engineers) to understand their friction points and requirements.
Oversee and have accountability for the successful definition and readiness of assigned project work within the platform domain.
Effectively facilitate technical workshops and planning sessions, demonstrating consensus-building skills among engineering groups with differing technical opinions.
Proactively focus on "Developer Experience" (DevEx)—identifying and eliminating impediments in the SDLC to maximize flow and velocity for feature teams.
Document complex technical requirements, system behaviours, and process flows (e.g., CI/CD pipelines, environment management) ensuring they are clear for implementation teams.
Build strong, trusted relationships within the Developer Platform team and with the wider Engineering community by making work transparent and managing change as technical issues arise.

What experience you’ll bring

Minimum of 3 years' experience as a Business Analyst, preferably within a technical domain, infrastructure, or platform team.
High familiarity with developer practices, SDLC processes, and technical jargon. You do not need to code, but you must be able to follow technical logic and understand the implications of architectural decisions.
Proficient skills in mapping technical workflows (e.g., release processes, environment promotions) and data models.
Previous experience or familiarity with AS/400 (RPG) environments is advantageous.
Ability to collaborate with engineering teams and bring together different technical and non-technical viewpoints to drive towards an optimal solution.
Strong problem-solving skills; comfortable managing complexity and ambiguity within legacy modernization or infrastructure projects.
Highly developed written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical constraints into business impacts for non-technical stakeholders.
Experience in supporting User Acceptance Testing (UAT) or technical verification phases.
Tertiary qualifications in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related discipline are highly regarded but not essential.
Previous experience working within regulated environments highly beneficial

Location

Close to a variety of public transport and easy parking options, the Toowong office has excellent end of trip facilities (including secure bike storage and showers), outdoor spaces and plenty of modern collaborative areas to work and connect with colleagues. 

Conveniently based at Toowong Village tower with views of the mountains and the Brisbane River, our location is only 4 km from the city and provides access to retail, restaurants and other amenities.

The benefits of working at A&G

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Work flexibility - with options to work from home two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone.
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.

About us

Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community.  

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Qantas, Virgin Money and Coles Insurance.

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.

Auto & General values individual differences and believes in fostering an inclusive culture that creates a great place to work for all.

A note from Auto & General to recruitment agencies: We politely ask that you avoid making any approaches or sending any unsolicited resumes to our Recruitment Team or Hiring Leaders across our business. Auto & General is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Internal applicants: Please ensure your manager or team leader is aware of your application. If this sounds like your next challenge, click on the APPLY NOW button and submit your application.

We are accepting referrals for this position. If you know someone who has the right skills & experience, please encourage them to apply and advise our recruitment team.

Company Description

BESIX Watpac is an Australian multi-disciplinary construction company, backed by a century of global expertise and financial strength. A wholly owned subsidiary of the award-winning international contractor BESIX Group, we specialise in complex construction across all sectors, including building and infrastructure projects. Our ethos is ‘building the best together’, and collaboration is at the heart of everything we do.

Job Description

Reporting directly to our Chief Executive Officer, the Group Digital & Innovation Manager role combines strategy with hands-on delivery to implement innovative technology and ways of working that drives operational excellence, agility, and sustainable growth.

You will be responsible for:

Defining a progressive digital and innovation strategy that reflects organisational priorities.
Developing a roadmap supported by effective stakeholder engagement.
Delivering forward-thinking digital solutions to enable efficiencies across operations and support functions.
Partnering with stakeholders to identify and deliver opportunities for innovation.
Leveraging data analytics and insights to inform strategic decisions.
Monitoring and identifying emerging technologies and trends that enable operational effectiveness and strengthen market distinction.
Qualifications

Key requirements of the role include: 

Established track record leading innovation, transformation, or strategic initiatives within large organisations.
Demonstrated experience in driving digital strategy.
Strong commercial acumen and analytical ability.
Project delivery capability including change leadership.
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills.
Additional Information

Along with a competitive remuneration package and long-term opportunities for career development, our employees enjoy access to the following benefits:

Wellbeing Days - three additional leave days per year.
Awards and Recognition Program, rewarding employees who bring our values to life.
Access to a wellbeing program including annual skin checks and flu vaccinations.
Corporate discounts to gym memberships, meal subscriptions and more.
Social events and Family Days.

 

At BESIX Watpac, we are committed to fostering a workplace where everyone is valued, supported and respected. By embracing all ages, gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, education and appearance, our people feel safe, empowered, and motivated to be their best.  If you’re excited about this role but don’t tick all the boxes, we encourage you to apply if you have transferable skills and believe you would be a great fit with BESIX Watpac.

Join our talented and passionate team delivering once-in-a-generation projects.

BESIX Watpac acknowledge the traditional custodians of the land on which we live and work, and pay respect to the Elders past, present and emerging.

GROUP CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

ASX listed financial services firm

No two days the same

Experienced Chief Financial Officer required

Finexia Financial Group Limited (ASX:FNX) is an independent financial services firm, specialising in private credit, funds management and advisory. Finexia was listed on the ASX in 2015 and has offices in Sydney and Surfers Paradise.

As a result of structural changes within the Group, we now seek to add Chief Financial Officer to our Gold Coast office located in Surfers Paradise.




What you will do

In this dynamic position, your expertise will be pivotal in enhancing the strategy and operations for our financial services group.




Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership: Partner with the CEO and Board to define and execute the Group’s financial strategy, capital management, short and long-term growth objectives.

Financial Reporting & Planning: Oversee the preparation and presentation of comprehensive financial reports, budgets, and rolling forecasts for all entities.

Strategic Analysis: Lead business modelling, risk assessment, financial due diligence and strategic initiatives.

ASX Compliance: Manage all ASX listing rule requirements, including half-year and annual reporting, continuous disclosure obligations, and regulatory filings.

Tax: Manage tax compliance and planning.

Audit: Coordinate external audits and planning.

Operational Finance: Enhance and automate financial systems and processes to drive efficiency. Oversee AP, AR & payroll functions.

Commercial Advisory: Provide high-level commercial insights on legal agreements, complex contracts, and strategic partnerships.

Capital Allocation: Drive cost-efficiency and performance metrics across the Group’s portfolio.

Investor Relations: Lead the Group’s investor relations strategy, managing communications with shareholders, conducting analyst briefings, and representing the company at any investor events.




Requirements

Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree in Finance or Accounting. CA/CPA is required.

Exceptional knowledge on AASB and IAS accounting standards.

Highly skilled tax effect understanding for consolidated group entities.

Experience: 5+ years of progressive finance experience, with a significant portion in senior leadership (CFO, VP Finance, or Head of Finance) within an ASX-listed environment.

Public Markets: Proven track record in public market reporting and corporate governance.

Technical Excellence: Exceptional financial modelling capabilities and the ability to translate complex data into actionable strategic insights.

Communication: A sophisticated communicator able to engage effectively with the Board, institutional investors, and stakeholders.

Legal Acumen: Proven ability to navigate, review, and interpret complex legal and regulatory contracts.

Mindset: An energetic, proactive, and entrepreneurial attitude with meticulous attention to detail and a "hands-on" approach to problem-solving.




Finexia is no ordinary firm. Things move fast here and we respond swiftly to new opportunities and challenges. Whilst we are somewhat flexible, this role is generally fulltime in the office.

Applicants are invited to submit a resume of no more than 4 pages. Please include a brief cover letter addressed to the CEO. This letter should showcase how you can contribute towards the success of Finexia. Applications without this cover letter will not be considered.

Applications close on 30 March 2026. Only candidates shortlisted for this role will be contacted.

Applicant introductions via recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

As a Research Analyst, you will play a central role in shaping insights, supporting client strategy and ensuring research governance across a growing and dynamic investment function. 

This is an exciting opportunity to work closely with investment specialists, portfolio managers, client teams and governance partners — contributing directly to the quality, clarity and influence of QIC’s research outputs. 

 

About the Role 

The Research Analyst provides centralised research support across LMG, helping to inform new mandates, overlay solutions, prospective client opportunities and technical advisory work. You will ensure research outputs are robust, well governed and aligned to client expectations and QIC’s risk and compliance frameworks. 

This capability is newly established and plays a critical role in freeing up investment teams from research demands, supporting new strategies and improving the scalability, quality and consistency of research activity. 

You will support the development of insights that influence client conversations, proposals and decision-making, while also helping uplift tools, processes and documentation across the broader group. 

 

About You 

You are naturally analytical, curious and structured in the way you approach research. You enjoy turning complex information into clear, actionable insights that support client outcomes and investment decisions. 

You have excellent written and verbal communication skills, strong attention to detail and the ability to work closely with technical and nontechnical stakeholders. You’re proactive, organised and able to manage competing priorities in a growing environment. 

An interest in financial markets, investment strategy, quantitative concepts, research governance or institutional investing will set you up for success.

About AGI – Australian Group Insurances Pty Ltd

AGI – Australian Group Insurances Pty Ltd is a leading provider of insurance and risk solutions across Australia. We deliver modern, secure, and customer focused digital platforms that support insurance partners and industry stakeholders.

About the Role

AGI is seeking an experienced Senior IT Business Analyst to join our Technology team, working fulltime from our Sydney CBD office. This role is responsible for analysing business requirements, defining functional specifications, and supporting the delivery of secure, compliant, and high-quality software solutions that align with AGI's strategic goals.

Key Responsibilities

Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders both internal and external to gather, document, and validate requirements

Identify gaps, edge cases, and dependencies within workflows

Translate high level workflow diagrams into detailed, structured documentation that enables developers to deliver with clarity and efficiency

Work onsite with developers, testers, analysts, and business teams to ensure requirements are well understood and implemented correctly

Facilitate requirements workshops and effectively engage with stakeholders to elicit, clarify, and document business and data requirements.

Support project teams throughout the SDLC to ensure solutions meet expectations and delivery timeline

Manage test planning, UAT preparation, and defect triage

Ensure requirements align with regulations, security standards, and AGI governance

Prepare documentation including BRDs, functional specs, process flows, and user guides

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels.

Required Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor's degree in IT, Business, Computer Science, or related discipline

Demonstrated experience as an IT Business Analyst working on complex system integrations or transformation projects

Minimum of 5-7 years of experience as an IT Business Analyst

Strong understanding of SDLC methodologies

Strong understanding of data modelling

Excellent analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills

Australian citizenship or permanent residency required

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in insurance, financial services, or regulated industries

Experience in Scrum or agile workflows

Experience with process modelling, data analysis, or integration projects

Familiarity with SQL and the ability to write queries would be advantageous.

Work Arrangement

This is a fulltime, onsite, in office role
Remote or hybrid work arrangements are not available

What AGI Offers

Competitive salary package

Stable, collaborative, in office environment

Opportunity to work on critical systems for the Australian insurance sector

Long term career development and growth

Senior Business Analyst - Payments - FTC

Location: Melbourne (remote)
Contract length: 12 months (definite extensions)
Salary: $130-160k + super

Role Overview

As a Business Analyst, you will play a key role in bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions. You will focus heavily on payments-related initiatives - including card systems, compliance, fraud, and system integrations, while also supporting broader digital transformation projects across core banking, CRM and digital channels.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and support payments-related initiatives with a focus on compliance, card security, Visa/Mastercard schemes, and fraud prevention.
Analyse and document business requirements for integrations across systems such as core banking, CRM, SMS gateways, data warehouses, and digital banking platforms.
Work closely with developers and architects to deliver API integrations and support microservices architecture (e.g., MuleSoft, middleware).
Ensure solutions align with the Banking Code of Practice, regulatory standards, and internal risk policies.
Translate complex technical concepts into clear business requirements and user stories.
Support end-to-end project delivery including business case development, stakeholder engagement, UAT planning, and change management.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including Product, Risk, Operations and Technology.

Required Skills & Experience

5+ years of experience as a Technical Business Analyst in the banking or financial services industry.
Demonstrated expertise in payments systems (NPP, BPAY, international payments), with a preference for deep knowledge in card payments, card security, and fraud detection.
Strong understanding of Visa/Mastercard ecosystems and compliance frameworks.
Proven experience working on API integrations, microservices, and middleware platforms (e.g., MuleSoft).
Familiarity with core banking systems, digital banking platforms, CRM tools, and data warehouses.
Strong knowledge of payments compliance and regulatory requirements in the Australian banking environment.
Experience with Banking Code of Practice and customer fairness principles.
Ability to work in agile environments and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, evolving setting.
Excellent communication, documentation, and stakeholder management skills.

If the above seems like a good fit, please apply and we will be in touch if your profile is suitable!

ECI is seeking a motivated results-oriented, National Channel Sales Manager. A strategic “hunter” hungry for generating new greenfield opportunities within the APAC region while maintaining current relationships. You will play a pivotal role in driving and growing our channel network exponentially by strengthening partnerships, developing new channels, and crushing annual quota. This position is focused on Business Intelligence (BI), Workflow Automation, and Data Collection & Reporting technologies, selling software-based solutions to partners and resellers within the APAC region.

You will help educate the market about ECI’s unique ability to transform an organization's efficiency with unique real-time insights and reporting tools into their most complex problems. Embrace value-based selling techniques and thrive in developing positive business outcomes. Our focus is quality of interactions and strength in relationships, not quantity. Strive to bring value to your selling motion and ensuring your customers are well-positioned to obtain a rapid time to value on their investment. Possess a strong understanding of the Challenger Sale, solution selling, and MEDDPICC principles. Natural ability to accurately forecast sales activity and successfully use Sales and CRM tools to close deals. To thrive in this role, you keep up with industry trends, create massive action, and have a passion for transforming customer relationships.

If you get excited about the future of sales technology, BI, workflow process automation, Software as a Service (SaaS), and solving complex C-suite and Board-level revenue problems in a fast-paced, hyper-growth environment, then join us at ECI! We have an award-winning company culture, based on employee feedback and are extremely proud to earn this award year over year. Experience the best of both worlds; encounter the dynamic feel and energy of a start-up coupled with a long and tenured record of success among a large & expansive base of customers. Come be an integral part of our diverse organization.

Do not miss the opportunity to make your mark in developing a sales channel of great strategic significance.

Expectations/Responsibilities

Conduct meetings and product demonstrations via remote sessions and in-person.

Identify, recruit and onboard new channel partners and resellers.

Build a pipeline and close net-new business through established channels.

Actively prospect and build a pipeline of new customers.

Lead the development of key deliverables such as proposals, SoWs, contracts, presentation materials, etc.

Lead client negotiations.

Manage sales activities of channel and reseller partners to generate revenue.

Provide leadership in the transition to delivery.

Coordinate with channel partners to create and execute business plans to meet sales goals.

Evaluate channel sales performance, recommend improvements and new verticals.

Educate channel about product portfolio and complimentary services offered.

Develop process improvements to optimize channel partner management activities.

Educate accounts on solution functionality, operation, and Tier I level support.

Some travel for trade shows or in-person meetings (~30%).


Requirements

Minimum 5 years’ experience selling software-based solutions focused on data collection and BI/Analytics into channels and managing partners/resellers.

Possess a demonstrated track record of success building and managing a pipeline and closing new business/achieving quota.

Coachable attitude.

Expertise in developing integrated technology-enabled business transformation propositions with supporting business cases.

Ability to work effectively across a variety of internal and external stakeholders.

Solid consultative sales experience, possess an in-depth understanding of business operations and workflow analysis, BI, process automation and alerting, data collection and reporting, and SaaS solutions.

Manage key client stakeholders and maintain effective partnerships.

Ability to effectively manage CxO and Board level relationships.

Highly developed verbal and written communication skills, develop and deliver high-quality product and sales presentations.

Demonstrates resilience, perseverance, learning mindset, tolerance to ambiguity, and possess a winning attitude despite challenges.

Excellent written and verbal communication to conduct business with executive level prospects.

Ad Text:
Employment Type: Full-time, continuing position
Remuneration: Competitive total package
Location: Kensington, NSW (Hybrid working model – 3 days in office)

About UNSW

At UNSW, we pride ourselves on being a workplace where the best people come to do their best work.  We aspire to be Australia’s global university, improving and transforming lives through excellence in research, outstanding education and a commitment to advancing a just society.

Why your role matters

Reporting to the Director of AGSM Executive Education, the Head of Product and Program Implementation provides senior leadership over the Navigator platform, product strategy, and learner success. The immediate priority is stabilising and optimising program implementation to ensure seamless transitions from contract to launch. By leading high-performing teams, this role champions the strategic reuse of learning assets and fosters a customer-centric culture that aligns delivery with evolving market needs.

This position requires a tech-savvy strategic thinker who can drive value-add efficiencies and innovation within the constraints of legacy systems. Beyond day-to-day management of three direct reports, the role is designed to elevate the unit’s strategic capability and scalability. The ideal leader will leverage technological advances to improve productivity and ensure deep integration across sales and operations, delivering high-quality, impactful learning experiences.

About the Role

You will be required to undertake and have experience in the following areas of expertise:

Prioritise the immediate stabilisation and optimisation of program implementation to ensure seamless transitions from contract handover to successful program launch.
Provide strategic leadership across the product portfolio and learner success, mentoring functional heads to build capability and align with long-term growth objectives.
Act as the business owner for the Navigator platform, leveraging tech advances and platform partnerships to drive functionality and modernise delivery within legacy constraints.
Champion the strategic reuse of learning assets and architectures to maximize operational efficiency, scalability, and return on investment.
Establish robust governance and consistent methodologies to ensure all programs are delivered on time, within budget, and to high-quality standards.
Foster a high-performance culture focused on innovation and value-add efficiency, representing the unit externally to strengthen market credibility and industry relationships.

About You

Tertiary qualifications or equivalent professional experience in a senior leadership capacity, ideally within a large, multi-disciplinary environment.
Proven track record in program and project management, with a sharp focus on the immediate stabilization and delivery of complex corporate or institutional programs.
Advanced people leadership skills to drive cultural change, value-add efficiency, and innovative thinking while navigating the constraints of legacy systems.
Strong business acumen and technical literacy, including the ability to act as a functional owner for learning delivery platforms and support technological scalability.
Exceptional stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and negotiate across diverse internal and external functions to ensure seamless contract-to-launch transitions.
Demonstrated expertise in governance, risk management, and process improvement to ensure all initiatives are delivered on time, on budget, and to high-quality standards.

For further information on the role & responsibilities, please refer to the Position Description

How to Apply: Submit your CV & Cover Letter detailing your interest and suitability for the job (as per the skills & experience bullet points in the job description) before Sunday 5 April by 11:55pm.

 

UNSW Benefits and Culture: People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world. Our benefits include: 

Flexible Working Options (work from home, flexible hours etc) 
Additional 3 days leave during December festive period.
Career development opportunities 
Up to 50% discount on UNSW courses
Flexible 17% Superannuation contributions, additional leave-loading payments and salary sacrifice.
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)

Job queries contact: Tai James, Talent Acquisition Consultant

E: tai.james@unsw.edu.au  (applications to the address cannot be accepted, please apply online)

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff.

 

Pre-Employment Checks

 

Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.

These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:-

 

National and International Criminal history checks
Entitlement to work and ID checks
Working With Children Checks
Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration
Verification of relevant qualifications
Verification of relevant professional membership
Employment history and reference checks
Financial responsibility assessments/checks.
Medical Checks and Assessments

Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW.

Key Points

$200k-250k p.a.

Minimum NV1 Security Clearance Required

Canberra-based role

Important

If you’re available to start in July 2026 for an initial 12-month term (with potential 12-month extensions), we encourage you to apply. There is also an immediate opportunity to join the team now, with a confirmed role through June 2026 and the possibility of extension beyond.

Overview

Our client is seeking an experienced Technical Capability Analyst to support a major Defence acquisition project. The successful candidate will work closely with Project Sponsor staff to develop key capability documentation, ensuring alignment with the One Defence Capability System (ODCS), reviewing documentation generated by Delivery Agencies, and facilitating capability traceability across all project phases. This role is pivotal in supporting pre‑Gate 2 activities, shaping industry engagement, and delivering new capability into service.

Essential Requirements

Demonstrated experience in requirements / specification development and project management within Defence capability environments.

Experience working within CASG or equivalent Defence acquisition organisations.

Proven ability to develop and maintain ODCS artefacts.

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including experience supporting ATM development and pre‑Gate 2 industry engagement.

Desirable Experience

Background in Defence systems engineering.

Experience in capability management.

Experience in the conduct of tender evaluation.

Additional Information 

Our preferred candidate will be able to tailor their approach to a dynamic capability project and work in a multi-disciplinary team. They should be comfortable working in both an independent and collaborative manner, and capable of managing multiple activities concurrently. 

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. 

Standard employee screening will be conducted including an Identity check, Residential History check, Eligibility to work in Australia check, Security clearance check, Sanctions/consolidated list check and a National Police check among others. 

We look forward to reviewing your application.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Lending Transformation (12-Month FTC)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support a large, strategic lending project focused on delivering an uplift to our home loan and lending capability. You will join the Lending team within the Experience Org and be dedicated to a major transformation and migration program, working closely with product, technology, operations and risk stakeholders to design and deliver high-quality customer, broker and team member outcomes.

This is a 12-month max-term contract, with the option to extend based on project needs and performance. 

Job Description


This is a hands-on role, focused on requirements, process design, data and operating model change in a fast-paced environment.

Lead end-to-end business analysis for complex lending initiatives, from discovery through to implementation and review.
Work across residential mortgage lending, platform and system change, core banking and servicing, including end-to-end mortgage operations.
Support transformation and migration activities, including data and process migration across platforms and providers.
Partner with technology, operations, credit, risk and compliance to deliver scalable, compliant solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Business Analysis & Requirements

Lead discovery, stakeholder workshops and define clear business requirements, user stories and acceptance criteria.
Translate complex needs into actionable artefacts for engineering, operations and vendors.

Process & Journey Design

Map current and future-state processes across origination, servicing and collections.
Identify opportunities for simplification, automation and improved customer/broker experience.
Ensure alignment with credit policy, regulatory obligations and risk controls.

Transformation & Migration

Support platform and operating model changes, including system migrations.
Contribute to data mapping, migration rules, reconciliation and cutover planning.

Delivery & Implementation

Work within cross-functional teams to prioritise backlogs and support delivery.
Support UAT, defect triage, test planning and readiness activities.
Track and communicate progress, risks and key milestones.

Risk, Compliance & Credit

Ensure alignment with regulatory requirements (e.g. NCCP, ASIC) and internal policies.
Embed credit rules, decisioning criteria and controls into processes and systems.

Stakeholder Engagement

Build strong relationships across business, technology and external partners.
Communicate complex topics clearly and support governance forums with concise insights and recommendations.

Qualifications


5+ years experience as a Business Analyst in financial services, with significant exposure to home loans / residential mortgage lending.
Demonstrated experience working on large transformation and/or migration programs, ideally involving core banking or lending platforms.
Hands-on experience with mortgage operations (e.g. settlements, servicing, collections, discharges, variations).
Experience working with or alongside risk, compliance and/or credit underwriting teams in a regulated lending environment.
Background in core banking systems, loan origination / servicing platforms or similar enterprise platforms is highly regarded.
Experience working in Agile or Hybrid delivery environments with cross-functional teams.

Nice to have

Experience in transformation programs involving third-party servicers or outsourced operations.
Exposure to APRA/ASIC regulatory change programs or risk transformation in a lending context.
Relevant qualifications in business, finance, information systems or related discipline

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
